reviews.review_description: JavierJb has been using hacks in two matches now. He's dealing 10 damage on every attack. Please analyze this player.

I love the original Demeo, it is one of my absolute favorite games, and I play it daily. When Demeo Battles came out, I was excited because I thought it was going to be another hit game like Demeo.

And to some degree it is but playing this game for any extended period reveals some real issues that lie under the surface. To put it bluntly this game has a lot of bugs in it and because it’s been released for a while now and these bugs still exist it’s looking like nobody cares enough to do anything about them.

Some notable ￼issues that I have experienced are:

Characters and lamps can occupy the same space which breaks the game making you unable to move. This just happened to me prompting this review.

Multi player mode breaks if you’re the host and your character dies, it acts like the game starts over and starts the opening sequence again with the bell ringer and the coin flip to see who goes first. Often this happens two times before you can resume play. Then when it’s your turn to finally go it won’t let you move.

There’s been other small glitches as well I’ve noticed that don’t break the game and are not worth going into here but man I really hope they dedicate like one person to fixing this. It kinda makes me not want to buy Battlemarked until I can be assured it won’t follow suit.

reviews.review_title: Good game, but they could have done more
reviews.review_description:
I really love this game. We started with the basic demeo and when they came out with this, we really fell in love with it. However, they really could have done more with the game. One of the nice things about the game is that, as you play, you get experience points and you can use those points to buy things in the game. It was fun having to purchase some of the better characters and then buying arenas. But those are going to be limited and I have so many extra keys and experience points and the only thing that I can buy with them are things like skins or tombstones and I'm really not as interested in those. I think it would have been so much fun if they offered the ability to buy special cards, monsters or special powers to use in a game. Even if they were just one time uses or limited time uses. In fact, it would have been really cool if, when you're purchasing items, one of the options would be to use one of your specially purchased cards or no monsters. 

Stop stop. I do also wanted to note that, as you will see in some of the other reviews, the game is a bit glitchy. But I find that the glitches come and go. Sometimes when we're playing, it's pretty glitchy and sometimes it gets a little annoying. But then if we come back another day, it's perfectly fine. It's a relatively new game, so I think they're still working out the kinks. 

I definitely do recommend it. It's a great game.

Demeo Battles has not worked all day. says online service unavailable 

Is there a fix

developer_response.body:
Hello there! The Online Service is now back online.
Thank you very much for letting us know!

reviews.review_title: When the devs are true gamers!
reviews.review_description:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
I love DB — it’s become a core part of my weekly hangouts and honestly one of my most-played titles on Quest. The mix of tactics, luck, and big moments makes it endlessly replayable, and it’s one of the few games I can fire up daily and still be surprised.

I also have to show big appreciation for the tilt option on the table as it keeps me playing for hours with zero neck pain. 👍

That said, I do think some heroes could use a little more love. Hunters and Sorcerers tend to dominate, while Bard, Warlock, and Barbarian don’t always feel as impactful. A few small, thematic tweaks could really balance things out:

Warlock: Spectral Possession — a repeatable ghost that possesses an enemy creature for 1 round (or heals 1HP to an ally). It’s disruptive without being OP.

Bard: Discordant Dirge — a song that limits enemy heroes to 1 movement point for 2 turns. Great for controlling tempo, not a hard stun.

Barbarian: Battle Rush — a repeatable +1 movement boost with a 3-turn cooldown. Helps him close gaps without breaking balance.


I’d also love to see more flexibility in private games:

A toggle for the lava floor, so players can choose whether to include it.

An option for neutral factions — creatures hostile to all players — to shake things up.


Finally, one big wishlist item: will you ever allow 4 heroes per team in co-op, so I could take two heroes and my ally could take two as well? That would take private matches to another level.

In summary: I’ve got a huge appreciation for DB and the devs behind it. It’s clear this is made by people who actually play their own games, and it shows in the balance, polish, and the fun. With a few extra tweaks, this could be the ultimate VR tactics experience.

Thanks for the great game!
happens probably 50% of the time i get lagged out and disconnected

Demeo and Demeo Battles are two of my favorite games. I will keep playing them for the rest of time I hope. It’s unfortunate that Resolution Games is the developer though because they are very slow to fix bugs and problems, do not respond to user feedback or bug reports, and just generally very bad at managing their games. There are issues in the game that have been begging for attention for years that will probably never be addressed so just be prepared for little annoyances that will probably never go away, but they are still amazing games. Just don’t get your hopes up that Resolution is going to do anything about any bug reports or problems you report.

UPDATE: Response to Developer Reply

It’s not lost on me that Resolution Games’ response to my review includes what appears to be a considerate question about my recommendations for improvement, making them look like they care, while in fact they know full well that I have no means of responding to that question in this format, hence this update to my original post. 

It is not accurate to say that you carefully review all of your feedback. I would put significant money down betting that ￼plenty of feedback goes straight into the trash. If it was carefully reviewed, then my submission about all of the bugs and problems in Demeo Battles would have been addressed by the correct team and applied to Demeo Battles. Instead of that happening, I got a response from one of your team members about the new Demeo update that was just released, that has apparently solved all of my reported issues. 

How does a different game getting an update solve the issues in the game I’m talking about? Your employee did not even read my submission or it would’ve been very evident which game I was talking about, and it landed on the desk of a member of the Demeo team because until very recently, after I pointed it out, you all had no category for Demeo Battles in your feedback form. 

I realize that whoever wrote this ￼response has a job to do and that’s to maintain that stellar public image, but I’m not having your reply on my review make you look better by pasting some standard corporate response that doesn’t mean anything. 

Instead, try actually doing better work and then￼ you won’t get reviews about how poorly managed your games are.

developer_response.body:
We truly appreciate all the feedback we receive from our players—it plays a big role in how we continue to improve the game. We get suggestions from many different channels, and while we carefully consider each one, we unfortunately are not able to implement them all.
We can not promise anything, but all feedback is reviewed and valued. What would be your top suggestion for "Demeo Battles"?
It’s fantastic to hear that you’re enjoying both "Demeo Battles" and "Demeo". Thanks for being part of our community—happy gaming!

developer_response.body:
Hello! The nature of the game is that the shop, chests and dice rolls will sometimes just not be in your favour. 

Since you describe unlimited cards and monsters however... If you genuinely believe you encountered a player using cheats, please make a detailed report on our helpdesk: https://resolutiongames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us and/or use the in-game reporting tools and we will investigate. Thank you!

Appreciate your patience, and thanks for sticking with us!

Hi there,

Thanks for your report!
We’ve looked into this and identified a bug that could cause characters to act multiple times unintentionally. This issue has now been identified and a fix is under way, so you shouldn’t see this behavior anymore after that.

Appreciate you flagging it and helping us improve the game!

placing a marker where on the board is actually possible for every player in order to show your partner where you believe the enemy is and coordinate attacks.

reviews.review_description: If you’re looking for a fun strategy board game that you can easily play with friends or solo this is a great option. I love the original Demeo, however, games can run several hours and this can be an inconvenience. With Demeo Battles games are much shorter - your turn is timed, and the board becomes smaller and smaller (burn areas appear that will start to damage your characters if they stand in them), forcing characters to come closer and closer together and preventing the battle from going on too long. Graphics have also gotten a boost which look really nice on the Oculus 3. There are new skins and maps as well. If you prefer a shorter game and are choosing between this and Demeo, I would recommend this. You have the option to add a CPU to your team or play against a CPU. You can also play online multiplayer games. Overall a fun spin off of Demeo.
The dice decide who wins, would be a great tactical game without dice, to bad dice ruin the game.

Save your money.  This game was awesome at first.  But they've clearly abandoned it to work on something else (dd) has gotten worse with bugs and connection errors every day, it's almost unplayable now and I'm noticing many long time players bailing. It's a shame.

developer_response.body:
Hi there!

we have identified an issue where the Quest SDK we use could cause unnecessary network lag. We will be updating to another SDK version so hopefully these issues will be better for you soon.

Thanks for reporting and playing Demeo Battles!

reviews.review_title: If you’re looking for a strategy game….
reviews.review_description:
If you’re looking for a strategy game this is NOT it. The game has the potential to be great.. but with the dice mechanic and random card selection, there’s little room for a strategy. If you calculate your moves and think outside of the box you will be hung out to dry when your opponent  rolls 3 double damage in a row, and you roll a skull(zero damage and miss). The only consistent and effective strategy is to hide behind minions with a bard and range hero and wait out your opponent. 95% of people will play this way and it gets BORING.
  Anyone on here that claims otherwise just enjoys the randomness because it makes up for their lack of skill. I am a top tier strategy gamer and I’m just trying to look out for those who are hoping for a strong strategy game. 
   If they offer a hardcore mode where it’s strictly move based and lost the dice mechanic the experience would be 200% better. 

To put it in a nutshell… imagine you’re playing chess and move your queen into check mate… but first you have to roll a dice that will determine NOPE and now you’re left with your pants down and your opponent that was being crush the whole time starts rolling double damage…that’s Demeo Battles.

Could be great. Just isn’t. The original Demeo is wonderful though! The mechanics make sense with a dungeon crawl and actually add to the experience.

If not would be 5 stars.

This game has so much potential but the stupidity the monster AI is horrific with the simple decisions it should be making. The hero's need to be better balanced, there is too much discrepancy and needs work.

This game has it all. They really listened to all the constructive feedback from the original and applied it to this game. 

Zero Complaints except the fact that I can't get enough of it!

reviews.review_title: Very Fun Concept but Lacks Polish
reviews.review_description:
Edit: The below review was written before the ranked mode update. Its a lot more fun now, but still has all the issues mentioned below. Revised from 3 to 4 stars.



I am a big fan of the original Demeo, which is fully fleshed out and deserves all the praise it gets. I think Demeo Battles is a game for people who enjoyed the original Demeo and always wanted to try a competitive PVP version. 

I think the core gameplay behind Demeo Battles is a lot of fun. You can pick a team of 2 heroes with cards and monsters and face off against other players. It's a fusion RPG board game plus card game. The game borrows the Battle Royal idea of an encroaching damage zone that pushes all players to the center. I have had a ton of fun with friends, but also a lot of frustrations that make this game hard to reccomend.

- There are no original characters, monsters, cards, or map assets. Everything is taken from the original game.

- There is no ranked PVP mode, casual, and private matches only. 

- Getting the full set of maps and heroes takes a week to unlock. Original Demeo had everything accessible right away. This puts new players at a competitive disadvantage right away.

- Game balance is out of wack. 2 months in now with only the smallest of changes.

- Large number of bugs. These range from annoying to severely crippling your ability to win the game with certain heroes. Dozens have been reported via the feedback portal and a handful have been addressed over 2 months. 

- Hackers running rampant. There seems to be no security built into the game. There are players with way more resources in casual matches than should be possible. 

I pre-ordered this game and was super excited and wanted to love it. I just can't ignore the way the current state of the game is right now.  I feel like they needed more time before launching to polish things up. 

With incremental updates, I do think the game will eventually be great. I look forward to that and will update my review here when I see the changes. 

I would still recommend this to die hard Demeo fans who don't mind waiting for updates.

I decided to buy this game after beating all the scenarios on Demeo several times. 

This game is good. 

But I have to agree with other commenters that, for single player mode, trying to unlock even the first few heroes is going to take way too long.

There is a feeling of having paid for the game but most of it being locked, forcing you to make up the numbers in multiplayer. Not nice.
Just isn’t fun game based on luck. Too many I win single cards.

Developer responseToday 

We're listening! Battles is a game where the available creatures and cards change from time to time; there are so many combinations, we can never truly tell which ones players will really like and which will be more 'eh' than ''yeah'. The recent change made to the Guardian was in response to many players expressing frustration at the Guardian being *too* survivable in the Burn. It definitely does change things, but there are still many ways to use her that keep her as a valuable choice. We look at balance often, so we will likely continue to make changes where we see they are needed to give a positive player experience. We are listening, and we do want players to enjoy the game.


READ IMMEDIATELY DEVS.

As someone who's been playing this game far more than the average person b*tching about survivability (Of a bloody TANK WHICH IS SUPPOSED TO BE ABLE TO TAKE MORE DAMAGE THAN A GLASS CANNON BUT DOESNT EVEN DO THAT NOW!!!!)..is insane.

There is so much more I can go on about that you fked up and I'm not even going to list it all out cause clearly wasting my time as you think your initial response is appropriate when it's not. 1*.

The original Demeo was a complete joy to play, with clever gameplay and was full of features.I had no idea they could take that formula and create this faster paced version. All the core mechanics are here but with a competitive / cooperative twist. I love joining up with my pal against the CPU or having the odd duel against each other. I was so impressed that single player challenges were included. 
Obviously Demeo fans will get this, but I highly encourage others to get both this and the original.  Perfect have for VR, board game enthusiasts and D&D fans.

reviews.review_title: PvP reliant on luck instead of skill.
reviews.review_description:
The game is fun but seriously hampered by a clunky mechanics and bugs, a d a system that relies on a weighted dice instead of skill.

The board system that forces you to look down or adjust your angle every match.

The match queue system relies on creating a code that you have to message a friend every 15minutes to party each match.

The dice system which makes a hero miss a turn compltis OK for PVE but makes PVP ultimately rely on luck rather than skill.

If things change for the better, I will update my review.

reviews.review_title: Great game but would like a few bits
reviews.review_description:
Demeo is one of the only vr games I keep coming back to, it's main issue was game length often exceeds battery capacity! This solves it by condensing the action down to 20 min sessions.

As much as I'd like extra dungeons for the base game I think battles could have a few improvements to make it just as good. Namely

Allow coop in the single player mode, it's fun having a swarm of spiders Vs rats!
Increase the resolution slightly, it looks a bit pixelated on the quest 3. QGO can solve it.
Add extra rule sets for private games (no lava, extra money, neutral minions or level boss, fog)
Alternative battles modes and maps, if 3v3 could be added then capture the flag or attack and defend style mode could be great
Alternative for the library, it looks a bit boring compared to the basement

This is a great game to compliment demeo, it won't replace it but it's a fun addition.

Huge Demeo fan. Demeo Battles is… very meh. You don’t control the monsters, so they tend to battle it out amongst themselves while you square off with the other heroes. I weep thinking of all the effort that went into Battles over the past year vs. the base game and more adventures (which we would have gladly paid for over and over… see Walkabout Mini Golf as example). That’s where the effort should have gone, IMO. This is more or less a misfire.
Very challenging strategy game

When you first start playing, it seems fun enough. Until you start seeing the glaring holes. They don't block out your opponents avatars, so any planning or strategy you do right in front of your opponent. You can turn global voice chat off. But it's not off by default. Stealth? Your opponent watches your avatar move the piece, and they know right where you are. Making the assassin the worst hero in the game.  Doesn't really matter though, because any strategy goes out the window when each hero is always 1 hit away from death. I just played 3 matches in a row where every time my opponent attacks me they critical hit, and I miss. There is no strategy when luck is such a massive part. It ends up being a coin flip. The problem is they literally just took the exact heros and gameplay from the original demeo, without properly balancing it for a fun multiplayer experience. It just leaves you massively frustrated when you miss your shot then both your heros get crit by a fireball. You can't come back from that, and pure luck just wasted your time and made you lose. This game needs a massive rework. It's its own game, so make it its own game. Give heros more health to enable strategy. Do basic things like hiding your opponent by default. Increase the die size, so back to back miss to crits are more rare. It happened almost every game and determines who wins. Balance the game for a multiplayer experience. Dont just copy the stuff you already had from the pve game. What you have here is a game that feels like it's strategic when you pick your units and cards, but ultimately gets determined by shear luck nearly every game. Probably won't get the rework it needs to be good.

Save some money and visit: 
bit,ly/KingBon
Replace the , with .

reviews.review_title: Basically the only turn based strategy
reviews.review_description: On the whole platform.

reviews.review_title: Loving it,great change of pace for pvp.
reviews.review_description:
25 percent off 
lin(ktr)(dot)ee/knickanity

For a discount link for this game and many others just type the below text into the top bar of an internet browser and select which games you want discount for.

h a v e 2 5 o f f . b i o . l i n k

If you’re looking at this game your most likely familiar with Demeo and are just looking for a VS mode to play against friends or randoms. I do think this should have probably been included in the main title but appreciate the VR market isn’t too big and it’s much harder for VR developers to make money than 2D gaming so do understand why they have probably segregated the game modes.

Its quick games which last 15-20min, it does have a single player mode or you can practice against AI, private play against friends or multiplayer with Randoms.

If you like the core game and want some additional content and a VS mode this is worth a purchase.

Such an amazing game! I love it! You can sit back and chill while you battle people online. You can interact with your enemy and join up with randoms or friends to work together or battle against another team. It’s my new favourite game.

Crashes WAYYYY too much for price ...never gave a bad review since I played VR....THIS GAME IS MY FIRST REVIEW AND MY ONLY BAD Experience Every Time I PLAY...ITS NOT MY INTERNET ITS NOT MY BRAND NEW HEADSET....ITS THIS LOW Quality OF A GAME....I'M EXTREMELY DISAPPOINTED ...I WANT A REFUND ...IVE GAVE IT TOO MANY CHANCES EVERYONE QUITS AN IT FREEZES

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you've had bad experiences with it - it sounds like you're experiencing crashes and disconnects?

reviews.review_description: If you like, absolutely outsmarting your opponent and have them right where you want them to then just have the game mess you over time and time again this game is for you. I’ve lost track of how many games I’ve lost because it was down to one move  and wiff. you will 90% of the time, If it’s down to one move, you will whiff. You’ll build a beautiful army, and then watch as the NPC steps away from the enemy into the fire, wasting their move and then moving back, literally doing nothing in a turn. You watch a turret that has clear line of sight to an opponent just not even fight. Please fix your game.

developer_response.body: I'm sorry to hear you're not having a good experience with the game; we are looking into issues with creatures not taking their turns as expected, we hope to be able to solve this soon. Our analytics don't show any issues with dice results being outside of the fair/expected ranges, but we will continue to monitor this.

reviews.review_description:
My back is against a wall & Burned. 

Make better Single player mode that's more like a campaign instead of just a tutorrical challenge, and cut that Burn out completely or reduce it way down its ridiculous.  I would love to give this a great review, but I'm really let down with this Version of Demeo since the OG Demeo by itself has a great community and the gameplay I is great. 

There's not much a I see it for achievements, Upgrades, Bragging rights, except when winning pvp battles. 

I understand that the mechanics was bent towards pvp and competivene, but it needs more for the single player also. 

I'll update my review accordingly if updates can make some of these wanted player changes.  

Devs Keep up the great work with Demeo & Demeo Battles and Future games like Dungeons & Dragons.

Devs Please Take these suggestions to heart to make Demeo Battles the Best it Can Be For All Gamers & Gaming Styles

Jason C

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback - we, too, are proud of the Demeo community, and I appreciate the thoughtful input you give. We have many updates and changes we wish to make, and we hope to bring as many as we can to the community to keep improving their experience of Battles.
